one_time_description: Tsikaristisere / Dundee Land Claim Settlement recognized as revenue net of research and legal costs of $4,272,284
economic_notes: Council operates a senior facility and chronic care facility with combined capacity of 80 beds. Operates ambulance services, water and wastewater plant, rental units, affordable housing/mortgage program. Akwesasne Harbour Development Corporation and Cornwall-Akwesasne Harbour Partnership are government business enterprises. Council has a $7,000,000 loan facility with BMO for community housing.
notes: Large one-time land claim settlement of $235,536,152 (Tsikaristisere/Dundee) settled January 19, 2020; net proceeds of $236,855,786 received April 6, 2020 (after fiscal year end). This dramatically inflates accumulated surplus to $412M from $160M. Infrastructure & Housing expenses of $9,472,654 reported under that segment. The annual surplus of $252,182,785 includes the land claim settlement; annual surplus before the land claim was $16,646,633. ISC funding not separately broken out from total government transfers in the income statement; deferred revenue shows ISC portion of $13,381,025. Pension expense $3,038,566. One consolidated subsidiary: Akweks:kowa Corp. Two GBEs on modified equity basis: Akwesasne Harbour Development Corporation and Cornwall-Akwesasne Harbour Partnership. Schedules 1-6 are unaudited supplementary information. Council is located in Ontario (Cornwall area) but territory spans Ontario/Quebec/New York.
